lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you in charge of this squad?

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Yes. We're guarding this entrance against demonic excursions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's that going?

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Not super well, I'm afraid. It's a nerve wracking post, and it's taking its toll on my soldiers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I do anything to help?

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Not in any official capacity. Making any sort of move might spark a chain reaction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But unofficially...

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: It would be good to know what's going on down there. If you can handle yourself, and you can keep an eye on the demons, you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: We just need to know if it looks like they're preparing for anything. But if you do this, you're on your own. If anything happens, I won't be able to send help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Understood. Well, if I see anything suspicious, I'll be sure to casually mention it.

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What wrong with your soldiers?

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Some of them are afraid, others impatient. That's the biggest problem actually. They're all having different reactions. It's making it difficult to work as a unit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do they usually have this problem?

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: No, they're good soldiers. I suspect it has something to do with those cultists and their little chats.

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Not that we can do much about that. We can't afford to initiate hostilities. There's a lot at stake, and I'm honestly not sure we could handle a direct confrontation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does sound like a difficult situation to be in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Lieutenant
lines.text: Goodbye.
